# Polygone Art

> Polygone Art is a community-run archive that preserves the free 3D assets from Google Poly, the 3D model library Google shut down on June 30th 2021. The goal is to keep this artwork available to artists, researchers, and the general public. The website (https://polygone.art) is an open-source, cookie-free single-page app that lets you browse, preview, and download the models, and the underlying data is mirrored for bulk download via torrent and zip archives.

## Licensing (read this first)

There are two distinct licenses in this project. Do not confuse them:

- **The 3D assets / artwork** (everything served from `blob.polygone.art`) is published under the **Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC-BY 4.0)** license: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/. You are free to use, remix, and redistribute the models commercially or non-commercially **as long as you credit the original author**. Each asset's author is identified by `authorId` / `authorName` in its metadata (see the data model below).
- **The website source code** in this repository is licensed under **ISC** (see `package.json`). It is hosted on GitHub at https://github.com/arodic/polygone.art and built with the [io-gui](https://iogui.dev/io/) framework.

When using any model, attribute the named author. When reusing the code, follow ISC.

## What this project is

- An archive (not the original service). Google Poly was shut down by Google; these assets were salvaged before the shutdown. Background: https://support.google.com/poly/answer/10192635 and https://killedbygoogle.com/.
- A static frontend (`index.html` + compiled `dist/index.js`) that fetches all data at runtime from the blob host. There is no application backend or database in this repo; the repo is the viewer, the data lives at `https://blob.polygone.art`.
- Privacy-respecting: no cookies. The app does fire lightweight `fetch` "pings" to the blob host for page/asset view counts (see `src/poly-app.ts` and `src/poly-model-view.ts`), but stores nothing in the browser beyond URL-hash state (current page, filter, grid size).

## Data model and endpoints

All data is served from the blob host. In the code this base URL is `BLOB_URL = 'https://blob.polygone.art'` (`src/poly-app.ts`). Assets are identified by an opaque `guid` string (the original Google Poly asset ID).

### Catalog: `https://blob.polygone.art/data/assets.csv`
A headerless, streamed CSV. Each row describes one asset:
`guid,authorId,name,tag1,tag2,...`
- Column 0: `guid` (asset id)
- Column 1: `authorId` (links to the artists list)
- Column 2: `name` (may be empty)
- Columns 3+: zero or more free-form `tags`
A model is considered a **Tilt Brush** painting if its tags include `tilt`; otherwise it is treated as a **3D mesh**.

### Inline thumbnails: `https://blob.polygone.art/data/thumbs.csv`
A headerless, streamed CSV of tiny preview thumbnails used for the grid: `guid,base64jpeg`. The base64 string omits a shared JPEG header that the app re-prepends at runtime (see `jpegHeaderData` in `src/poly-gallery.ts`); it is not a complete standalone JPEG on its own.

### Artists: `https://blob.polygone.art/data/users.csv`
A headerless CSV mapping each author to their work: `authorId,authorName,assetGuid1,assetGuid2,...`.

### Per-asset metadata: `https://blob.polygone.art/assets/{guid}/data.json`
Full JSON record for a single asset. Fields used by the viewer (`src/poly-model-view.ts`) include:
- `name`, `description`
- `authorId`, `authorName` (use these for CC-BY attribution)
- `createTime` (timestamp)
- `tags` (array of strings)
- `presentationParams.backgroundColor` (suggested viewer background)
- `formats`: array of available export formats. Each entry has a `formatType` (e.g. `GLTF2`, `OBJ`, `FBX`, `TILT`) and a `root.relativePath` pointing at the entry file. The web viewer renders the `GLTF2` format.

### Asset files and thumbnails
- Rendered model file: `https://blob.polygone.art/assets/{guid}/GLTF2/{root.relativePath}`
- Preview images: `https://blob.polygone.art/assets/{guid}/thumbnail-{size}.jpg` where `size` is one of `32`, `64`, `128`, `256`, `512`.
- Per-format download archive: `https://blob.polygone.art/archives/{guid}/{guid}_{formatType}.zip`

### Browsing taxonomy (UI filters)
- Type filter: `all` (All Models), `tilt` (Tilt Brush), `3d` (3D Mesh).
- Category filter (matched against tags/name): `animals`, `architecture`, `art`, `culture`, `food`, `history`, `nature`, `objects`, `people`, `scenes`, `science`, `tech`, `transport`, `travel`.

## Bulk download of the full archive

For mirroring or offline/research use, prefer the torrent over hammering the blob host:
- Torrent: https://blob.polygone.art/torrent/polygone.torrent
- Split archive parts: https://blob.polygone.art/torrent/polygone/polygone-split.z01 ... `.z15` plus https://blob.polygone.art/torrent/polygone/polygone-split.zip
- Compressed assets and data directories: https://blob.polygone.art/torrent/assets.zip and https://blob.polygone.art/torrent/data.zip

## Notes for automated agents

- Treat `blob.polygone.art` as a static, read-only content host. There is no write API.
- Always carry author attribution (`authorId` / `authorName`) alongside any model you ingest, so downstream use can satisfy CC-BY.
- For large-scale ingestion, use the torrent/zip bulk downloads rather than crawling per-asset endpoints.
- CSV files are headerless and streamed; rows may arrive in chunks split across network reads, so buffer partial trailing lines when parsing.
